inclusionRule: Includes open-source multi-agent frameworks, orchestration platforms, and control planes with at least 50 GitHub stars at snapshot time. Nexus Prime is included as the focal baseline even though the star threshold applies to the comparison set rather than the product being evaluated.

tiers.label: Tier 1 · Direct control planes / developer orchestrators
tiers.description: The closest group to Nexus Prime: repo-adjacent control planes and developer orchestration layers that shape how coding agents execute, verify, or collaborate.

tiers.label: Tier 2 · Broader multi-agent frameworks / platforms
tiers.description: Multi-agent frameworks and platforms that help teams build agentic systems, but are usually broader application frameworks rather than a repo-first control plane.

tiers.label: Tier 3 · Adjacent / specialized frameworks
tiers.description: Qualifying projects with a narrower substrate, domain, or execution posture that still overlap with multi-agent orchestration decisions.

repos.name: OpenHands
repos.audience: Developers who want a general autonomous software-development agent
repos.orchestrationModel: AI-driven development agent platform for planning and executing coding tasks
repos.executionSubstrate: Managed development runtime for browsing, coding, and tool execution
repos.memoryContextModel: Task and session context centered on the active development run
repos.operatorSurface: Web app, CLI, SDK, hosted and self-hosted surfaces
repos.bestAt: General autonomous development workflows and issue-to-code execution
repos.tradeoff: Less centered on a bootstrap-first MCP control plane for external coding clients

repos.name: Ruflo
repos.audience: Claude and Codex teams that want packaged multi-agent coordination
repos.orchestrationModel: Swarm-oriented orchestration platform with predefined coordination surfaces
repos.executionSubstrate: Agent swarms, autonomous workflows, and packaged orchestration modules
repos.memoryContextModel: RAG integration and self-learning swarm context
repos.operatorSurface: CLI, configs, orchestrator packages, client integrations
repos.bestAt: Packaged multi-agent coordination with strong swarm scaffolding
repos.tradeoff: More topology-centric than a repo-first control plane with persisted runtime truth

repos.name: Shannon
repos.audience: Engineering teams that want production-oriented multi-agent orchestration
repos.orchestrationModel: Production-oriented orchestration with approval, observability, and multiple execution strategies
repos.executionSubstrate: Framework-managed multi-agent runs and production workflow policies
repos.memoryContextModel: Context governance and production execution state
repos.operatorSurface: CLI, framework runtime, docs, and operational controls
repos.bestAt: Production-minded orchestration with strong approval and observability posture
repos.tradeoff: Less focused on MCP-first client bootstrap and dashboard-led runtime truth

repos.name: Metaswarm
repos.audience: CLI power users working across Claude Code, Gemini CLI, and Codex CLI
repos.orchestrationModel: Self-improving multi-agent orchestration scaffold with TDD and quality gates
repos.executionSubstrate: CLI-oriented development harness and command-driven agent flows
repos.memoryContextModel: Spec, task, and improvement-loop context
repos.bestAt: Spec and TDD-oriented multi-agent coding flows
repos.tradeoff: Lighter runtime truth and operator surface than a fuller control plane

repos.name: kata-orchestrator
repos.audience: Spec-driven engineering teams
repos.orchestrationModel: Agent orchestration for spec-driven development
repos.executionSubstrate: Task and spec pipeline around coding agents
repos.memoryContextModel: Spec, task, and workflow context
repos.bestAt: Spec-driven development loops and repeatable task breakdown
repos.tradeoff: Narrower operator surface than a broader control plane

repos.name: multi-agent-squad
repos.audience: Claude Code teams that want specialized agent squads and Git automation
repos.orchestrationModel: Specialized multi-agent development orchestration with automated workflow steps
repos.executionSubstrate: CLI plus Git-oriented development automation
repos.memoryContextModel: Execution context around squad tasks and automation state
repos.operatorSurface: CLI, repo automation, and squad scaffolding
repos.bestAt: Claude Code-centric development automation with specialist agents
repos.tradeoff: More toolchain-specific and narrower than a general MCP control plane

repos.name: AssemblyZero
repos.audience: Developers who want configurable multi-agent development routing
repos.orchestrationModel: Parameterized multi-agent orchestration framework for coding agents
repos.executionSubstrate: CLI-oriented harness with configurable orchestration parameters
repos.memoryContextModel: Task and session context around the configured agent flow
repos.bestAt: Fast configurable routing across coding agents
repos.tradeoff: Smaller control-plane and persistence surface than Nexus Prime

repos.name: Nexus Prime
repos.audience: Agent builders and operator-heavy coding teams
repos.orchestrationModel: Bootstrap-first MCP entry that routes into one orchestrator-owned execution contract
repos.executionSubstrate: Local repo plus worktree-backed runtime and verifier lanes
repos.memoryContextModel: Persisted memory fabric, session-first RAG gate, and source-aware token budgeting
repos.operatorSurface: MCP, dashboard, runtime truth APIs, generated client bootstrap
repos.bestAt: Repo-grounded coding-agent operations with runtime truth and operator visibility
repos.tradeoff: More opinionated than lightweight scaffolds that only expose agent harnesses or SDK primitives

repos.name: MetaGPT
repos.audience: Teams exploring software-company-style agent collaboration
repos.orchestrationModel: Multi-agent framework built around role-specialized software-company metaphors
repos.executionSubstrate: Framework-managed agent roles and collaborative task flows
repos.memoryContextModel: Role, artifact, and project context
repos.bestAt: Software-company-style multi-agent collaboration
repos.tradeoff: Heavier role topology and less control-plane discipline for external coding clients

repos.name: AutoGen
repos.audience: Developers embedding agents into products or internal tools
repos.orchestrationModel: Programming framework for agentic AI and multi-agent applications
repos.executionSubstrate: SDK and framework runtime inside application code
repos.memoryContextModel: Framework-managed conversation, state, and workflow context
repos.bestAt: Building agentic applications and custom multi-agent systems
repos.tradeoff: Framework-first, so teams still assemble their own repo/runtime control plane

repos.name: CrewAI
repos.audience: Teams that want role-based autonomous agent crews
repos.orchestrationModel: Role-playing autonomous AI agents working as crews
repos.executionSubstrate: Framework and platform surfaces for crew execution
repos.memoryContextModel: Agent and task context inside crew flows
repos.operatorSurface: Framework APIs, docs, and hosted product surfaces
repos.bestAt: Crew-style task orchestration and role-based agent collaboration
repos.tradeoff: Less focused on runtime truth and repo-grounded coding execution

repos.name: ChatDev
repos.audience: Teams exploring collaborative multi-agent software work
repos.orchestrationModel: LLM-powered multi-agent collaboration for software tasks
repos.executionSubstrate: Framework and platform surfaces for collaborative agent work
repos.memoryContextModel: Project and artifact context across collaborating agents
repos.bestAt: Collaborative software-agent experiments and workflows
repos.tradeoff: Less focused on a local-first control plane for external coding clients

repos.name: LangGraph
repos.audience: Application teams building long-running stateful agents
repos.orchestrationModel: Graph-based orchestration for resilient language agents
repos.executionSubstrate: Stateful graph runtime inside app stacks
repos.memoryContextModel: Checkpointed graph state and durable execution context
repos.operatorSurface: SDK, docs, and LangGraph platform tooling
repos.bestAt: Durable graph-based agent workflows
repos.tradeoff: Lower-level framework that requires more assembly around operator surfaces

repos.name: CAMEL
repos.audience: Researchers and builders exploring large-scale agent systems
repos.orchestrationModel: Multi-agent framework oriented around large-scale agent experimentation
repos.executionSubstrate: SDK, framework runtime, and research-oriented modules
repos.memoryContextModel: Agent memory and task state patterns inside the framework
repos.bestAt: Broad multi-agent experimentation and scaling work
repos.tradeoff: Less opinionated about repo-grounded runtime operations

repos.name: Swarms
repos.audience: Teams wanting a broad multi-agent orchestration surface
repos.orchestrationModel: Enterprise-grade production-ready multi-agent orchestration framework
repos.executionSubstrate: Framework runtime across multiple agent orchestration modes
repos.memoryContextModel: Framework-managed agent state and orchestration context
repos.bestAt: Broad orchestration coverage and flexible swarm patterns
repos.tradeoff: Breadth over the tighter repo-first discipline of a control plane

repos.name: Agency Swarm
repos.audience: Teams wanting reliable multi-agent orchestration around agency patterns
repos.orchestrationModel: Reliable multi-agent orchestration with structured agency communication
repos.executionSubstrate: Framework runtime and agent communication flows
repos.memoryContextModel: Agent thread and task context
repos.bestAt: Structured agency patterns and directed agent collaboration
repos.tradeoff: Less local-first runtime truth and client bootstrap surface

repos.name: BotSharp
repos.audience: .NET teams building AI agents and multi-agent systems
repos.orchestrationModel: AI multi-agent framework in .NET
repos.executionSubstrate: .NET runtime and framework stack
repos.memoryContextModel: Bot, task, and application context
repos.bestAt: .NET-native agent systems and multi-agent applications
repos.tradeoff: Different stack and fewer repo-first MCP surfaces

repos.name: LLMStack
repos.audience: Teams building agent workflows and apps without heavy code investment
repos.orchestrationModel: No-code multi-agent framework for workflows and applications
repos.executionSubstrate: Platform and workflow builder with data integration
repos.memoryContextModel: App and data context managed by the platform
repos.operatorSurface: Web platform, docs, and hosted workflows
repos.bestAt: No-code and low-code agent workflows with business data
repos.tradeoff: Less repo-grounded developer control-plane depth

repos.name: agentUniverse
repos.audience: Developers who want to build multi-agent apps quickly
repos.orchestrationModel: LLM multi-agent framework for building applications
repos.executionSubstrate: Framework runtime and application-building toolkit
repos.memoryContextModel: App and agent context inside the framework
repos.bestAt: Quick multi-agent application assembly
repos.tradeoff: Less operator-facing runtime truth than Nexus Prime

repos.name: Arbiter
repos.audience: Teams focused on design, simulation, and auditing use cases
repos.orchestrationModel: Multi-agent framework for design, simulation, and auditing
repos.executionSubstrate: Framework runtime for evaluation-heavy agent flows
repos.memoryContextModel: Simulation and audit context
repos.bestAt: Design, simulation, and audit-heavy scenarios
repos.tradeoff: More specialized than a general coding-agent control plane

repos.name: AIEvo
repos.audience: Developers building multi-agent apps in the Ant ecosystem
repos.orchestrationModel: Multi-agent framework for creating multi-agent applications
repos.executionSubstrate: Framework runtime and application-building toolkit
repos.memoryContextModel: Application-level task and agent context
repos.bestAt: General multi-agent app construction in that ecosystem
repos.tradeoff: Smaller community and less operator surface than larger frameworks

repos.name: Yacana
repos.audience: Builders who want task-driven multi-agent primitives
repos.orchestrationModel: Task-driven LLM multi-agent building blocks
repos.executionSubstrate: Framework primitives and composable orchestration blocks
repos.memoryContextModel: Task-driven context within the framework
repos.bestAt: Composable multi-agent primitives
repos.tradeoff: More building blocks and fewer operator-facing control-plane surfaces

repos.name: ContextAgent
repos.audience: Builders who want a context-central multi-agent API
repos.orchestrationModel: Context-central multi-agent framework with a PyTorch-like API
repos.executionSubstrate: SDK and framework runtime for context-centric systems
repos.memoryContextModel: Central context model as the coordination layer
repos.bestAt: Context-centric agent composition
repos.tradeoff: Less opinionated about runtime operations and developer control planes

repos.name: Snowflake Orchestration Framework
repos.audience: Snowflake teams building agents around Snowflake services
repos.orchestrationModel: Multi-agent framework with native Snowflake support
repos.executionSubstrate: Snowflake-native services and orchestration stack
repos.memoryContextModel: Snowflake data and execution context
repos.operatorSurface: Framework docs and Snowflake-oriented integration points
repos.bestAt: Agent systems that live close to Snowflake
repos.tradeoff: Much narrower substrate than a general coding-agent control plane

repos.name: nexus-agents
repos.audience: Builders who want distributed multimodal agent orchestration
repos.orchestrationModel: Distributed multi-modal agent orchestration in a microservices architecture
repos.executionSubstrate: Microservices-based orchestration across multimodal services
repos.memoryContextModel: Distributed processing state across services
repos.bestAt: Distributed multimodal pipelines
repos.tradeoff: Different problem shape than a repo-grounded MCP control plane
